sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Granite Construction stock hits all-time high at 122.85 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Granite Construction Inc. (GVA) stock reached an all-time high of $122.85, reflecting a 38.58% appreciation over the past year. This surge is supported by investor confidence, 36 consecutive years of dividend payments, and a "GOOD" financial health score, despite InvestingPro suggesting the stock may be overvalued. The company recently exceeded Q3 2025 EPS expectations, secured a $24 million contract, and declared a quarterly dividend.

feed.title: Emergent BioSolutions Announces $100 Million Paydown of Term Loan Principal, a Significant Step Achieved Toward its Multi-year Transformation
feed.summary: Emergent BioSolutions announced a voluntary prepayment of $100 million on its term loan facility in late December 2025, using cash on hand. This action reduces the company's gross debt by $275 million since 2023, totaling $593 million, representing a 32 percent decline since the start of its multi-year transformation plan. This move aims to improve Emergent's financial position, enhance flexibility, and support long-term stability and growth.

feed.title: Will Enphase Energy (ENPH) Beat Estimates Again in Its Next Earnings Report?
feed.summary: Enphase Energy (ENPH) has a history of consistently beating earnings estimates, with an average surprise of 28.23% over the last two quarters. Analysts are optimistic about its next report, giving the company a positive Zacks Earnings ESP of +10.63% and a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold), suggesting another earnings beat is likely. Investors are advised to utilize the Earnings ESP Filter for informed decisions.

feed.title: Forum Energy Technologies appoints Leslie Beyer to board of directors
feed.summary: Forum Energy Technologies, Inc. (NYSE:FET) has appointed Leslie A. Beyer to its Board of Directors. Beyer, who previously served at the U.S. Department of Interior and as CEO of the Energy Workforce and Technology Council, will also serve on the company's Compensation & Human Capital Committee and the Nominating, Governance & Sustainability Committee. Her extensive government and industry experience is expected to bring valuable insights to Forum Energy Technologies.

feed.title: FIS closes Global Payments’ Issuer Solutions acquisition
feed.summary: Financial technology firm FIS has announced the completion of its acquisition of Global Payments’ Issuer Solutions business, formerly known as TSYS. The deal was valued at $13.5 billion, with a net purchase price of $12 billion after accounting for tax assets. The transaction closed ahead of schedule, marking a significant move for FIS in the financial technology sector.

feed.title: CVRx expects 11-18% revenue growth in 2026 following CPT code transition
feed.summary: CVRx, Inc., a medical device company, projects its 2026 revenue to be between $63 million and $67 million, marking an 11-18% increase, following the implementation of Category I CPT codes for its Barostim therapy. The company also announced strong preliminary Q4 and full-year 2025 results, expanded its debt facility to $100 million, and received FDA approval for its BENEFIT-HF trial design. Analysts maintain a consensus "Buy" recommendation, indicating positive momentum despite the company's cash burn for growth initiatives.

feed.title: BNP Paribas Exane Adjusts KKR & Co Price Target to $160 From $162, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: BNP Paribas Exane has adjusted its price target for KKR & Co. to $160, down from $162, while reaffirming an "Outperform" rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst other analyst actions, including Rothschild & Co Redburn raising its price target to $167 and Barclays lowering its target to $159, both maintaining positive ratings. KKR & Co. is a financial services group primarily involved in insurance and asset management, with significant assets under management.

feed.title: Live Nation NZ Confirms Bank of New Zealand as Naming Rights Partner for Waikato Regional Theatre
feed.summary: Live Nation New Zealand has announced a five-year naming rights partnership with Bank of New Zealand (BNZ) for the Waikato Regional Theatre, which will now be known as BNZ Theatre. The 1,300-seat venue in Hamilton is set to reopen in January 2026, aiming to become a major cultural hub and drive economic benefits for the Waikato region. The theatre will host diverse programming, including performances by prominent artists like TEEKS, Sir Dave Dobbyn, Bic Runga, and Sophie Ellis Bextor.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Highlights Procter & Gamble's Recent Rally
feed.summary: Jim Cramer highlighted Procter & Gamble's recent rally and discussed its performance within the consumer packaged goods sector, noting its resilience compared to weaker operators. He previously recommended PG as a company with a strong dividend history, suggesting a dollar-cost averaging strategy for investment due to its current undervaluation and potential for change with a new CEO. While Cramer acknowledges PG's value, the article also notes the potential for greater upside in certain AI stocks, specifically mentioning an undervalued AI stock benefiting from Trump-era tariffs and onshoring.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Says “Cardinal Is Really the Monster” Among Drug Distributors
feed.summary: Jim Cramer highlighted Cardinal Health (NYSE: CAH) as a strong performer among drug distributors, referring to it as "really the monster" of the group. The company, along with Cencora and McKesson, saw over 40% growth last year. Brown Advisory also noted Cardinal Health's benefits from rising demand for specialty therapies and its strong execution in the medical distribution business.

feed.title: Deadline Alert: F5, Inc. (FFIV) Shareholders Who Lost Money
feed.summary: Glancy Prongay & Murray LLP is alerting shareholders of F5, Inc. (FFIV) who suffered losses to contact them regarding a securities fraud lawsuit. The lawsuit stems from two significant drops in F5's stock price following disclosures of a "highly sophisticated nation-state threat actor" gaining unauthorized access to company systems and subsequently providing low growth expectations for fiscal 2026. Investors who purchased F5 securities between October 28, 2024, and October 27, 2025, have until February 17, 2026, to file a lead plaintiff motion.

feed.title: Deadline Alert: Gauzy Ltd. (GAUZ) Shareholders Who Lost Money Urged To Contact Glancy Prongay & Murray LLP About Securities Fraud Lawsuit
feed.summary: Glancy Prongay & Murray LLP is reminding investors of a class action lawsuit against Gauzy Ltd. (GAUZ) with a lead plaintiff deadline of February 6, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that Gauzy made materially false and misleading statements during the Class Period between March 11, 2025, and November 13, 2025, concerning the financial health of its French subsidiaries which subsequently entered insolvency proceedings, causing a significant stock price drop. Investors who suffered losses are encouraged to contact the firm to learn more about recovering their losses.

feed.title: FTC Blocks Edwards Lifesciences’ $945M JenaValve Acquisition
feed.summary: The Federal Trade Commission has blocked Edwards Lifesciences' $945 million acquisition of JenaValve Technology, stating the deal was not in the public's best interest. Edwards Lifesciences disagrees with the decision and has revised its full-year 2026 adjusted earnings per share guidance downward as a result. The company plans to provide additional updates during next month's fourth-quarter earnings call.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Says International Business Machines “Is Still Very Inexpensive”
feed.summary: Jim Cramer highlighted International Business Machines (IBM) as one of his "old tech" favorites, noting that it is still very inexpensive despite significant runs in its stock price. He mentioned that Cisco Systems, another legacy tech giant, has also shown strong performance. Cramer emphasized that both IBM and Cisco, which is part of his Charitable Trust, continue to perform well, with IBM trading at record highs but still considered cheap and a buy.

feed.title: Justices Seek US Views on General Dynamics’ No-Poach Suit
feed.summary: The US Supreme Court has requested the solicitor general's input on a case concerning "no-poach" agreements involving General Dynamics and other shipbuilders. The antitrust case, revived by a divided Fourth Circuit ruling, alleges an unwritten agreement since the early 1980s to not recruit each other's naval engineers, which purportedly suppressed wages and limited professional opportunities. A former naval engineer is representing thousands in this class action.

feed.title: Why Capital Bancorp is splitting CEO roles at bank and parent
feed.summary: Capital Bancorp, Inc. (NASDAQ: CBNK) has announced significant executive leadership changes, renewing CEO Ed Barry's employment and elevating Steve Poynot to CEO of Capital Bank, N.A. This restructuring aims to align the company's management with its long-term growth strategy as a diversified financial services enterprise. The company reported $3.4 billion in assets as of September 30, 2025.

feed.title: Co-Diagnostics announces 1-for-30 reverse stock split By Investing.com
feed.summary: Co-Diagnostics, Inc. (NASDAQ:CODX) will effect a 1-for-30 reverse stock split on January 2, 2026, to meet Nasdaq's minimum bid price requirement. The company's stock has plummeted 69.44% year-to-date, and it reported significant revenue decline in Q3 2025. Despite financial challenges, Co-Diagnostics continues to expand its technology, recently securing an Australian patent for its Co-Dx PCR Pro platform and receiving a "Buy" rating from Maxim Group.

feed.title: DTE Energy Advances on Clean Energy Initiatives & Investments
feed.summary: DTE Energy is making significant capital investments to modernize its infrastructure, expand clean energy initiatives, and enhance service efficiency for customers. The company plans to invest $30 billion over the next five years, with a focus on renewable generation and energy storage, targeting 2,900 MW by 2042. Despite these growth catalysts, DTE faces financial risks due to its substantial long-term debt and challenges in its Energy Trading business.

feed.title: Western Digital stock climbs even as Wall Street wobbles — what to know now
feed.summary: Western Digital shares climbed roughly 2% despite a weaker broader market trend, driven by investor interest in AI infrastructure spending and its reorganization to focus on hard disk drives for cloud data centers. Storage rivals Seagate, Micron, and SanDisk also saw gains. Investors are currently watching upcoming US inflation figures and major bank earnings reports for cues on the market's next move, amid concerns about policy risk and central bank independence.

feed.title: Howmet Aerospace to buy hardware business for $1.8B, among other M&A deals to end 2025
feed.summary: Howmet Aerospace is set to acquire Consolidated Aerospace Manufacturing for $1.8 billion, expanding its fastener portfolio and providing significant tax benefits. This acquisition is part of several manufacturing M&A deals occurring at the end of 2025, including Samsung Biologics' $280 million purchase of a U.S. drug production site and Deere & Co.'s acquisition of construction tech company Tenna to enhance its technology services. These strategic moves aim to expand operations, broaden product offerings, and strengthen market presence.

feed.title: Why Alphabet (GOOGL) stock is up today: Google shares jump over 1% as Alphabet becomes the fourth company
feed.summary: Alphabet (GOOGL) shares surged over 1% on January 12, 2026, pushing its market capitalization beyond $4 trillion for the first time, making it the fourth company to reach this milestone. This rally is primarily driven by a multi-year AI partnership with Apple, making Google's Gemini the AI backbone for Siri, along with strong Google Cloud growth and successful Gemini 3 model rollout. The company has also surpassed Apple in total market value for the first time since 2019, reflecting renewed investor confidence in its AI and cloud strategies.

feed.title: Tractor Supply opens 2,400th store in Aiken, South Carolina By Investing.com
feed.summary: Tractor Supply Company (NASDAQ:TSCO) has opened its 2,400th store in Aiken, South Carolina, as part of its expansion strategy to launch 100 new stores by the end of 2026. The new location features a 3,000 square foot Garden Center and complements an existing store in the area. The company, a Fortune 500 retailer with a market cap of $27.07 billion, also made a $2,400 donation to Aiken Equine Rescue during its grand opening festivities.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Launches a New Family of x86 Chips
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) has launched a new family of x86 chips, Ryzen AI Embedded, designed for AI applications at the edge. These processors, including the P100 and X100 Series, integrate a high-performance CPU (Zen 5), GPU (RDNA 3.5), and NPU (XDNA 2) into a single chip suitable for compact and power-constrained environments across various industries. Wall Street analysts maintain a bullish outlook on AMD, with several reiterating Buy ratings.

feed.title: Alphabet Inc. (Class C): The Quiet Engine Powering Google’s Next Decade
feed.summary: Alphabet Inc. (Class C) (GOOG) is highlighted as a critical investment vehicle for global exposure to Google's AI, cloud, and advertising empire. This non-voting share class offers pure economic exposure to Alphabet's diversified engines, including Google Search, YouTube, Google Cloud, and AI initiatives, without diluting founder control. The article underscores Class C's competitive advantages in data scale, integrated AI infrastructure, and multiple monetization streams compared to rivals like Microsoft, Apple, Amazon, and Meta.

feed.title: Caterpillar stock hits all-time high at $627.75 By Investing.com
feed.summary: Caterpillar Inc. stock has reached a new all-time high of $627.75, reflecting a significant 78.62% increase over the past year and a 53.15% surge in the last six months. The company's market capitalization stands at $293.26 billion, and it maintains a 56-year dividend payment streak. Recent developments include CEO Joseph E. Creed's upcoming appointment as Chairman and new AI integration initiatives.

feed.title: Blackstone moves to exit U.K. rental home platform Leaf Living - report (BX:NYSE)
feed.summary: Blackstone is reportedly looking to sell its U.K. rental home business, Leaf Living, for approximately £1 billion ($1.3 billion). Goldman Sachs and CBRE Group have been engaged to advise on the potential sale. The report, citing Bloomberg, indicates Blackstone's intention to exit the platform.

feed.title: Capital One $425 million depositor settlement wins preliminary approval following earlier rejection
feed.summary: A federal judge has granted preliminary approval to Capital One's revised $425 million class action settlement for depositors who claimed they were denied high interest rates. This approval comes two months after an initial rejection and includes a commitment from Capital One to pay higher interest rates going forward for 360 Savings accounts, a benefit valued at approximately $530 million.

feed.title: Norfolk Southern Corp.: Can This Rail Stock Get Back On Track After A Rocky Year?
feed.summary: Norfolk Southern Corporation (NSC) has shown signs of recovery after a challenging year marked by regulatory scrutiny and operational issues. The stock has seen modest gains over recent months and a positive, albeit understated, return over the last year. Wall Street analysts largely maintain a "Hold" to "Moderate Buy" stance, acknowledging improved operations and stabilizing freight demand but remain cautious due to lingering regulatory uncertainties and capital expenditure demands.

feed.title: Moderna expects 2025 revenue of $1.9B, above guidance
feed.summary: Moderna anticipates 2025 revenue of approximately $1.9 billion, exceeding its previous guidance. The company also raised its GAAP operating expense outlook for 2025 and reiterated its plan for up to 10% revenue growth in 2026, driven by COVID vaccine sales and potential new vaccine launches. Despite the positive revenue guidance, shares of Moderna traded down in early afternoon trading.

feed.title: Barclays Raises American Airlines Group (NASDAQ:AAL) Price Target to $16.00
feed.summary: Barclays has increased its price target for American Airlines Group (NASDAQ:AAL) from $12.00 to $16.00, while maintaining an "equal weight" rating. This new target suggests a marginal upside of 1.68% from the stock's previous close. The airline reported strong Q3 earnings, beating estimates with an EPS of ($0.17), and provided positive guidance for Q4 2025 and fiscal year 2025.

feed.title: FAT Brands receives Nasdaq delisting notice after stock falls below $1
feed.summary: FAT Brands has received a delisting notice from Nasdaq because its stock price consistently fell below $1.00 for 30 consecutive days. The company has 180 days to regain compliance by having its stock close above $1.00 for 10 consecutive days, or it will face delisting. This development follows previous notices of debt acceleration and a declaration by the company that bankruptcy was a possibility.

feed.title: Forum Energy Tech Appoints Leslie Beyer to Board
feed.summary: Forum Energy Technologies (FET) announced the appointment of Leslie A. Beyer to its Board of Directors, effective January 12, 2026. She will serve on the Compensation and Human Capital Committee and the Nominating, Governance and Sustainability Committee, receiving standard director compensation including restricted stock. An analyst rating on FET stock is currently a Hold with a $33.00 price target, while TipRanks’ AI Analyst, Spark, rates FET as Neutral due to strong technical momentum and positive earnings call guidance, balanced against financial challenges and valuation concerns.

feed.title: Citigroup Adjusts Price Target on Teledyne Technologies to $604 From $567, Maintains Neutral Rating
feed.summary: Citigroup has increased its price target for Teledyne Technologies (NYSE:TDY) to $604, up from $567, while reiterating a Neutral rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst other analyst updates, with Barclays also recently adjusting its price target for Teledyne. The article provides an overview of Teledyne Technologies, including its product segments and geographical sales distribution.

feed.title: Thomas Tesh, Rollins chief admin officer, sells $143k in stock
feed.summary: Rollins Inc.'s Chief Administrative Officer, Thomas D. Tesh, sold 2,396 shares of company stock for $143,903 on January 9, 2026, through a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. Despite the sale, Tesh retains significant direct and indirect holdings in the pest control company. Rollins has shown strong performance with a 33.25% stock increase over the past year, consistent dividend payments for 55 years, and notable gross profit margins, according to InvestingPro data and recent analyst reports.

feed.title: Citigroup Adjusts Howmet Aerospace Price Target to $255 From $246, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: Citigroup has increased its price target for Howmet Aerospace (HWM) shares to $255 from $246, while maintaining a Buy rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects Citigroup's updated outlook for the aerospace and transportation engineered solutions provider. The company, which specializes in engine products, fastening systems, engineered structures, and forged wheels, recently saw several other analysts also adjust their price targets.

feed.title: FTAI Aviation Announces the Launch of FTAI Power: FTAI Adapts the World’s Largest Aircraft Engine Platform to Meet AI-Driven Power Demand
feed.summary: FTAI Aviation has launched FTAI Power, a new initiative to convert CFM56 aircraft engines into power turbines to address the growing demand for electricity, particularly from AI-driven data centers. Leveraging its extensive CFM56 engine fleet and maintenance capabilities, FTAI aims to provide flexible, cost-efficient, and scalable 25-megawatt power solutions. Production is expected to begin in 2026, with the company projecting the capacity to deliver over 100 units annually.

feed.title: PayPal's Agentic Commerce Expansion: Will it Boost Top-Line Growth?
feed.summary: PayPal recently announced a strategic partnership with Microsoft to integrate Copilot Checkout into Copilot, enabling seamless shopping experiences. This is part of PayPal's broader push into "agentic commerce" through collaborations with various platforms like OpenAI, Perplexity, Wix, and Shopify, aiming to leverage AI for enhanced discovery and checkout processes. Despite these initiatives, PayPal's shares have declined 22% in the last six months, though the stock appears undervalued with a Value Score of A and its 2026 EPS is projected to grow by 9.7%.

feed.title: PayPal's Agentic Commerce Expansion: Will it Boost Top-Line Growth?
feed.summary: PayPal is expanding its agentic commerce initiatives through strategic partnerships with Microsoft, OpenAI, and Perplexity, integrating its payment services into AI-driven platforms. These collaborations aim to enable seamless shopping experiences within AI interfaces, allowing merchants to convert product catalogs into purchasable items. Despite these efforts, PayPal's stock has underperformed, though its valuation appears attractive, with analysts expecting future EPS growth.

feed.title: CenterPoint shares tips for saving energy as colder weather to return
feed.summary: CenterPoint is advising Indiana customers on energy-saving strategies and assistance programs as colder temperatures are predicted to return. This comes after December 2025 experienced one of the coldest two-week periods on record. Tips include adjusting thermostats, checking for drafts, and replacing furnace filters, alongside various financial assistance and billing options.

feed.title: Parnassus Value Equity Fund Exits Ball Corp, Impacting Portfolio by -2.01%
feed.summary: The Parnassus Value Equity Fund, managed by Billy Hwan, made significant portfolio adjustments in Q4 2025, according to its N-PORT filing. The fund, known for its fossil-fuel-free and positive workplace investment philosophy, completely exited its positions in Ball Corp and Comcast Corp, with Ball Corp's exit impacting the portfolio by -2.01%. Concurrently, the fund added new positions in Waste Management Inc and CMS Energy Corp, and increased stakes in companies like The Home Depot Inc and Linde PLC.

feed.title: Tractor Supply opens 2,400th store in Aiken, South Carolina By Investing.com
feed.summary: Tractor Supply Company has opened its 2,400th store in Aiken, South Carolina, a move reflecting its ongoing expansion strategy to add 100 new stores by the end of 2026. The new location features a 3,000 square foot Garden Center and held a community celebration including a $2,400 donation to Aiken Equine Rescue. Investors are looking ahead to the company's earnings report on January 29 for further details on its growth progress, while recent analyst upgrades and dividend increases suggest a positive outlook despite market fluctuations.

feed.title: Assessing ConocoPhillips (COP) Valuation After Recent Share Price Softness
feed.summary: ConocoPhillips (COP) has experienced a recent dip in its share price, sparking investor interest. Despite this short-term softness, analysts generally view COP as undervalued, with a consensus price target significantly above its current trading price. The valuation narrative highlights potential margin and earnings power changes, while acknowledging risks such as execution on large projects and fluctuating oil and gas prices.

feed.title: A Look At General Electric (GE) Valuation After Q3 Beat And US$1.4b Turboshaft Engine Contract
feed.summary: General Electric (GE) is under scrutiny after its GE Aerospace division reported strong Q3 results and secured a US$1.4 billion turboshaft engine contract. While a detailed narrative suggests the stock is 5.3% undervalued with a fair value of $339.69, driven by aerospace growth and improved free cash flow, another perspective points to its current P/E ratio of 42.1x as being rich compared to industry averages, indicating potential valuation risk. Investors are urged to consider both the growth potential and the elevated multiples.

feed.title: ICICI Bank Completes 100% Acquisition of ICICI Prudential Pension Funds Management
feed.summary: ICICI Bank Limited has acquired 100% shareholding in ICICI Prudential Pension Funds Management Company Limited from ICICI Prudential Life Insurance Company Limited. This acquisition makes the pension fund manager a wholly-owned subsidiary of ICICI Bank. The move is expected to enhance integration within ICICI Bank's financial services ecosystem and strengthen its presence in the long-term retirement and pension savings market.

feed.title: Vici, GLPI both get analyst thumbs-up
feed.summary: Jefferies Equity Research analyst David Katz has issued "Buy" recommendations for Vici Properties (VICI) and Gaming & Leisure Properties Inc. (GLPI), citing their stable business models. While acknowledging some current dynamism and risks, particularly for Vici's lease with Caesars and GLPI's involvement in major Bally's projects, Katz believes both REITs are long-term investments worth having. He adjusted Vici's price target to $43 per share and GLPI's to $62 per share.

feed.title: Palo Alto Networks Introduces New Vibe Coding Security Governance Framework
feed.summary: Palo Alto Networks' Unit 42 has released SHIELD, a new security governance framework designed to address vulnerabilities arising from "vibe coding," the practice of using AI prompts to generate code. While acknowledging the productivity gains of vibe coding, the report highlights new security risks such as data breaches and arbitrary code injection due to inadequate governance and lack of visibility. The SHIELD framework outlines step-by-step best practices including separation of duties, human oversight, input/output validation, and leveraging security-focused AI helper models to mitigate these emerging threats.

feed.title: Citigroup Adjusts Leidos Price Target to $232 From $218, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: Citigroup has increased its price target for Leidos Holdings (LDOS) to $232, up from the previous $218. The firm reiterated its Buy rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a positive outlook for Leidos, a company specializing in engineering, technological, and scientific services across various sectors.

feed.title: Experienced Team With $120 Million in Assets Joins Ameriprise Financial for Enhanced Client Experience and Operational Efficiency
feed.summary: The Loveday Caruso Wealth Management Group, managing $120 million in assets, has moved from LPL Financial to Ameriprise Financial. The Colorado-based team, led by Sean Loveday and Sarah Caruso, cited Ameriprise's client-centered technology, comprehensive experience, and strong home office support as key reasons for the switch. They anticipate significant growth and improved client engagement through Ameriprise's resources.

feed.title: Lululemon says it expects Q4 sales and EPS to be at high end of guidance
feed.summary: Lululemon announced it expects its fourth-quarter sales and earnings per share to reach the high end of its previously issued guidance. This positive outlook suggests stronger than anticipated performance for the athleisure retailer during the recent quarter.

feed.title: Low Volatility Is One Reason To Look At Options On This Fund
feed.summary: The S&P 500 recently reached a new record high, continuing gains from a bull market that began in March 2020. This sustained growth and the current extremely subdued volatility make the SPDR S&P 500 Trust (SPY) ETF an attractive option for investors considering options strategies. The article suggests that low volatility could be a key factor for those looking into options on this fund.
